title: Heavy rain alert in 6 Rajasthan districts today: Monsoon likely to weaken from tomorrow; mercury may stay a... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
- Monsoon Weakens In Rajasthan; Heatwave Continues | July Weather Alert
Heavy rain alert in 6 Rajasthan districts today:Rainfall 13% above normal so far, monsoon weakens in several districts; mercury crosses 42°C
- Copy link
Monsoon made its entry in Bikaner, Jaisalmer, Sri Ganganagar, Hanumangarh and Phalodi areas of Rajasthan on Thursday. However, this entry was weak.
Except for Hanumangarh, the weather remained clear in the remaining districts. Meanwhile, light rain occurred in Ajmer, Baran, Karauli and Dholpur in eastern Rajasthan. There is a yellow alert for storms and rain in 6 districts on Friday.
Meanwhile, weather experts said that the phase of monsoon rains in the state has now weakened. The weak phase of monsoon will begin from July 11. Meanwhile, in July, temperatures above 40°C are being recorded in many districts.
Photos related to weather in the state
These cities are the hottest
Major monsoon updates
Where the most rainfall occurred: In the past 24 hours, 49MM rainfall was recorded in Kekri of Ajmer, 28MM in Anta of Baran, 24MM in Surot of Karauli, 20MM in Siddhmukh of Churu, 10MM in Maslpur of Karauli, and 10MM in Pilani of Jhunjhunu. Meanwhile, light rainfall occurred at some places in Hanumangarh, Jalore, Bhilwara, Dausa, Dholpur, Jhalawar, Kota, and Sikar.
Temperature rose in Sri Ganganagar, Churu, and several other cities: With the weakening of the rainfall spell and the emergence of sunshine, temperatures have increased in several districts, including Sri Ganganagar, Churu, Jaipur, Alwar, and Chittorgarh. The highest temperature in the state was recorded at 42.5°C in Sri Ganganagar.
What will the weather be like ahead?
Weather in major cities of Rajasthan
Jaipur: The weather in the capital remained mostly clear throughout Thursday. Clouds appeared for a while, but then the sun came out. The maximum temperature in Jaipur rose by 1 degree and was recorded at 34.1°C on Thursday, while the minimum temperature remained at 25.7°C.
Alwar: Clouds remained overcast throughout the day in the district on Thursday. Humidity persisted, and there was no rain. A day earlier on Wednesday, Alwar had received good rainfall, with Ramgarh recording the highest rainfall of about 60 mm. Here the maximum temperature was recorded around 38°C.
Jodhpur: Cloud movement continued throughout the day. According to the Meteorological Department, the maximum temperature here was recorded at 35.3°C, which was 1.9 degrees below normal. Meanwhile, the minimum temperature was recorded at 28.8 degrees Celsius, which was 1.1 degrees above normal.
Udaipur: Despite the Meteorological Department's yellow alert, there was no rain in the district throughout Thursday. Clouds remained in the sky for some time in the morning, but after that the weather remained clear throughout the day, and bright sunshine prevailed, causing people to face humidity. The maximum temperature was recorded at 32°C and the minimum temperature at 24°C.
Sikar: The district experienced heat throughout Thursday. Bright sunshine prevailed from Thursday morning, although light winds blew in the evening amidst light clouds. Light clouds remained over Sikar district headquarters and surrounding areas, causing humidity. A slight increase in day temperature was observed. The maximum temperature was recorded at 36.2°C and the minimum temperature at 25.9°C.
Kota: People got relief from the scorching heat amidst the movement of clouds throughout the day. Here, the maximum temperature was recorded at 35°C and the minimum temperature at 25.2°C. According to the Meteorological Department, the temperature remains normal. However, there is a possibility of rain in the coming days as the monsoon becomes active.
